mainEntity.name: My boss is pressuring me to keep working even though I'm sick—can they fire me for filing a claim?

acceptedAnswer.text: Retaliation for filing a claim is illegal, but "at-will" laws make these situations tricky. Document all pressure from management and consult an attorney if you fear your job is at risk due to your illness.
acceptedAnswer.description: Under Utah and Idaho law, it is illegal for an employer to fire or demote you simply for filing a workers' compensation claim. This is considered "retaliatory discharge." However, because these states are "at-will," employers often try to cite other reasons like "performance issues" or "downsizing." If you feel pressured to work through a worsening illness, document every conversation and save every email. Your health is the priority, and ignoring a "work-related cough" can lead to permanent lung damage. If the retaliation is clear, you may have a secondary legal claim beyond just workers' comp. Having an attorney handle the communication often stops the harassment immediately.
